page.content: The first table presents the distribution of students by ethnicity, with "Ethnicity" as the header and "Percentage of Students" as the second header. The row labeled "White British" shows a representation of 53.5%. The "White ‘Other’" category has a percentage of 0%. For "Mixed/multiple ethnic groups," the percentage is 6.9%. The "Asian/Asian British" category accounts for 29.3%, while "Black/African/Caribbean/Black British" represents 1.7%. Finally, "Other ethnic group" has a percentage of 8.6%. The second table details the inhibition of xanthine oxidase activity with "Extracts" as the header and "Inhibition of xanthine oxidase activity IC50(mg/ml)" as the second header. The row labeled "Petroleum ether extract" indicates a value of "-", signifying no inhibition.

cells.cell_text: Inhibition of xanthine oxidase activity IC50(mg/ml)

page.content: The "Chloroform extract" shows an IC50 value of 1.77 mg/ml. The "Ethyl acetate extract" has an IC50 of 0.27 mg/ml. The "Methanol extract" registers an IC50 of 0.50 mg/ml, while the "TOF extract" presents an IC50 of 0.28 mg/ml. Together, these tables provide a comprehensive overview of ethnic diversity among students and the varying inhibition levels of xanthine oxidase activity across different extracts, highlighting significant differences in both educational demographics and biochemical properties.
